titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library Exhibition Materials
Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library Exhibition Materials
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday May 21, 2019 at 03:01

did.unittitle: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library exhibition materials

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library
Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four groupings: Living Portraits: Miscellaneous Exhibition Materials, 1939-1966, undated; Carl Van Vechten's Color Photographs of African Americans, 1939-1964, 2009; Devotion and Inspiration: Beinecke People, 2013; and In the Mind's Eye: Beinecke Architecture Imagined, 2013.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of signage, photographs, labels, and reproductions of collection materials created for Beinecke Library exhibitions.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library Exhibition Materials. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material is open for research.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization. Various acquisitions associated with the collection have not been merged and organized as a whole. Each acquisition is described separately in the contents list below.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library Exhibition Materials is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, 2013.

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs, labels, and signage relating to various Beinecke Library exhibitions.

did.unittitle: Signage for "Alfred Stieglitz: A selection of his papers"
Signage and labels for "Paul Rosenfeld, '12: Voyager in the arts, a memorial exhibition"
Sign for "James Purdy: His published work"
Photographs of "An Exhibition of Manuscripts, First Editions, and Photographs of Gertrude Stein"
Signage, labels, and photographs of "Sinclair Lewis, 1885-1951: A selection of manuscripts from the collection established by the author, class of 1907"
Signage and labels for "Wallace Stevens: An exhibition of his writings, 1998-1954"
Signage and labels for "H. D.: An exhibition in honor of her seventieth birthday"
Signage and labels for "Marianne Moore: An exhibit to observe her seventy-fifth birthday"
Living Portraits: Carl Van Vechten's Color Photographs of African Americans

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Prints of color transparency portraits by Carl Van Vechten created for the 2009 Beinecke Library exhibition, "Living Portraits." Includes copies of the exhibition catalog and labels.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged into two groups: framed portraits and unframed portraits.

did.unittitle: Pearl Primus, as a dancer in Hard Line Blues, 1943
Carl Van Vechten and Diahann Carroll, June 26 1955
Carl Van Vechten in Harlem on "Josephine Baker Day," May 20 1951

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Labels, signage, and display materials for the Beincke Library's 50th Anniversiary exhibition "Devotion and Inspiration: Beinecke People."

did.unittitle: In the Mind's Eye: Beinecke Architecture Imagined

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Labels, signage, and display materials for the Beincke Library's 50th Anniversiary exhibition "In the Mind's Eye: Beinecke Architecture Imagined."
